framework.reasoning: Caduceus is released on the HuggingFace Hub (kuleshov-group org: caduceus-ph_seqlen-131k_d_model-256_n_layer-16, caduceus-ps_*, etc.) with custom modeling code (trust_remote_code=True). Its core is the Mamba selective-SSM block, whose official forward pass depends on the fused CUDA kernels in mamba-ssm + causal-conv1d, so a GPU (A100/A6000/T4) is required for the real path. The reproduction fine-tunes the official pretrained weights on Genomic Benchmarks (mean-pool + linear head), matching Sec 5.2.1. YAML config for per-task hyperparameters. A pure-PyTorch BiMamba/MambaDNA reference implementation is also shipped so the architecture (RC equivariance, weight tying) can be unit-tested on CPU without the CUDA kernels.

module_hierarchy.description: Reference PyTorch implementations of the RC operation, BiMamba (weight-tied bi-directional), and MambaDNA (channel-split RC-equivariant wrapper), plus RC-equivariant embedding and LM head. Used for unit-testing the equivariance property (Thm 3.1) on CPU; the fine-tuning path uses the official HF weights.

Loads the official Caduceus backbone from the HF Hub (AutoModel, trust_remote_code=True) and attaches a mean-pool + linear classification head per Sec 5.2.1. Handles Caduceus-PS channel-split averaging (RC-invariance) and Caduceus-Ph post-hoc conjoining (RC-ensemble at inference).

module_hierarchy.description: Char-level DNA tokenizer (A/C/G/T/N + special tokens) matching the Caduceus recipe, with a reverse_complement helper for RC data augmentation and conjoining.

public_methods.signature: encode_batch(self, seqs: List[str], max_len: int) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]

module_hierarchy.description: Genomic Benchmarks loader (default) with optional RC data augmentation; task registry maps task -> (num_classes, metric).
Fine-tuning loop: AdamW, cosine schedule, early stopping on val metric, 5-fold CV support (Sec 5.2.1 / App D.1). RC data augmentation toggle for Caduceus-Ph.
YAML loader + seeding + Genomic Benchmarks task registry (metric + num_classes per task).

tensor_flows.name: Downstream classification forward (Caduceus-Ph)
steps: Caduceus backbone (BiMamba stack) -> hidden [B, L, D]
inference only: also run on RC(seq), average the two logits (post-hoc conjoining)

runtime.reason: AutoModel + trust_remote_code for Caduceus custom modeling code
selective-scan CUDA kernel for the official Caduceus forward pass
fused causal conv kernel required by mamba-ssm

entrypoints.purpose: Fine-tune Caduceus on a Genomic Benchmarks classification task
Evaluate a checkpoint on the test split (with optional post-hoc conjoining)
Download Genomic Benchmarks datasets via API

risk_assessment.description: The official Caduceus forward pass depends on mamba-ssm + causal-conv1d fused CUDA kernels, which need a CUDA GPU and can be finicky to build (must match torch/CUDA versions). Will not run on CPU-only.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Target a CUDA GPU (Colab T4/A100). Ship a pure-PyTorch BiMamba/MambaDNA reference for CPU unit tests of the architecture. Notebook installs mamba-ssm/causal-conv1d with pinned wheels and degrades gracefully to the reference path for the equivariance demo.

risk_assessment.description: Genomic Benchmarks tasks are grid-searched over LR {1e-3, 2e-3} per task (Table 5); a single run must fix one point (SIR conf 0.7).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default lr 1e-3, exposed in config with # ASSUMED comment; reproduce one task (human_nontata_promoters / human_vs_worm) rather than all 8.

risk_assessment.description: Exact HF repo id / variant (ph vs ps, seqlen, d_model) must be confirmed at load time.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Loader takes model_name from config; hidden size read from model config, never hardcoded; tries the 1k-seqlen small checkpoint by default for fast fine-tuning.

risk_assessment.description: Caduceus-PS channel-split averaging vs Caduceus-Ph conjoining differ; using the wrong post-processing hurts accuracy.
risk_assessment.mitigation: variant flag in config drives pooling/conjoining logic; default Caduceus-Ph (best overall on Genomic Benchmarks).
